evidence: Default constructor and conditional are direct; package tests and normal AppState construction use Gateway::new without any production invariant that revocation must be configured.
confirmed for Gateway::new without with_revocation_path: the only current snapshot block is skipped
proof_gap: No authoritative shipped binary wiring exists in this crate, so final deployment prevalence must be confirmed by the parent scan.

source: Caller presents an otherwise valid, unexpired token that is revoked only in the current signed RevocationSnapshot.
closest_control: fabric_token::verify rejects an embedded Revoked status, and configured snapshots are verified/freshness-checked; however Gateway::new defaults revocation_kv_path to empty and AppState::new Production does not require enabling it.
sink_or_broken_control: Anthropic message non-stream skips snapshot lookup entirely when the default empty path is retained and proceeds to provider execution.
impact: A killed token, subject, tenant, lineage, service, identity, or trust-bundle version can continue inference until expiry in a default-constructed production-profile application.

attack_path.attacker: holder of a token revoked after issuance
preconditions: application uses default empty revocation path
token remains signed/unexpired and embedded status is not Revoked
attack_path.closest_control_failure: revocation is opt-in even though AppState defaults to Production
severity_facts: deployment precondition is the default constructor
